title: betula platyphylla var. japonica
intro: Betula platyphylla var. japonica, also known as Japanese white birch, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its white, papery bark and slender branches. The medicinal properties of Japanese white birch lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, similar to aspirin. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating conditions such as arthritis and gout. Japanese white birch is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and can also be made into a salve or tincture for topical application.
